name: neural-weights-en-us
model_lineage: v3.11.0-deploc-feed step-8000 ("feed-8k") — init_from v385 (6.6.0) step-8000, same-vocab fine-tune, `--resume none`, 8k steps (2k probe → 8k resume), LR 1.0e-5 constant. ONE escalation over the probe-2 checkpoint (v3.10.1-gb-probe2): dependent_locality training mass split across FOUR locales (GB 6.1% + NZ 2.4% + ES 3.0% + FR-lieudit 1.8% of stream; combined ≈6.66%, matching probe-2's 6.6% total but at a MUCH lower max single-locale share — 20.2%→6.1% — the diversity fix for probe-2's skew costs) instead of GB alone. THREE resurrection levers, bundled (each alone is a known no-op per #456/#1100): (1) `reinit_label_rows: [B-dependent_locality, I-dependent_locality]` — mean-of-live re-init of the baked-dead classifier rows; (2) `classifier_learning_rate: 1.0e-3` — a dedicated hot Adam param group on `classifier.*` (Adam's update is scale-invariant, so only a real param group moves a baked-negative row); (3) `class_weights` B-/I-dependent_locality 0.3→1.0 (Task 4a's third suppressor: re-init + hot LR are useless under a downweight). Tokenizer v0.9.0-multisplice (73,143) UNCHANGED; every lexicon UNCHANGED; architecture identical. Weights-only vs 6.6.0.
phase: STAGED, NOT PROMOTED (6.7.0 candidate) — ship checkpoint feed-8k operator-ratified 2026-07-23 over the sibling feed-2k checkpoint (Task 7's recommendation: feed-2k fails FR-fragment bare-locality outright, 0.665 vs ≥0.90; feed-8k's own miss — digit bare-street-hn — is narrower and covered by the digit-gate revision below). BLOCKED ON A NEW FINDING (Task 8, 2026-07-23): the Gauntlet's metamorphic layer FAILS on feed-8k — a NEW INV[comma-drop] violation on '1600 Pennsylvania Ave NW, Washington DC 20500' (comma-dropped form loses the rooftop resolution entirely, resolving to 0,0; v385 holds this case). The SAME layer independently FAILS on feed-2k too, with DIFFERENT violations (BAND[num-ordinal]/INV[comma-drop]/INV[abbrev] all mis-resolving '350 Fifth Avenue, New York, NY' 283.5km away) — this reads as a lineage-wide v3.11.0-deploc-feed regression in NY/DC-class admin resolution robustness, not a single-checkpoint fluke. Regression (33/33) and held-out (z=0.00, not significantly worse) layers PASS on feed-8k; only metamorphic fails. This is a SHIP-BLOCKING gate per the recipe's own pre-registration ('SHIP GATE: … gauntlet PASS') and was not caught by Tasks 6/7 (neither ran the Gauntlet). Every other pre-registered guard passes (see eval.gate_2026_07_23_v3110_deploc_feed_8k below) — promotion awaits the operator's triage of this one finding.

training.corpus_version: v0.15.0-deploc (v0.14.0-gb base + synth-nz-v2 + synth-es-pedania + synth-fr-lieudit) — the FOUR-locale dependent_locality feed. synth-gb-v1 (800k rows, 32.8% B-dependent_locality) at source weight 10.0 (down from probe-2's 36.0 — the diversity fix), synth-nz-v2 at 4.0 (79.2% dep-loc density), synth-es-pedania-v1 at 5.0 (30.3% density), synth-fr-lieudit-v1 at 3.0 (100% density — every row IS a lieu-dit). overture-latam (CA/MX, 6.6.0) unchanged at weight 6.0.
training.recipe: v3.11.0-deploc-feed.yaml — init_from v385 (6.6.0) step-8000, `--resume none` REQUIRED (reinit_label_rows lives in the init_from branch; --resume auto silently no-ops it), LR 1.0e-5 constant, 8k (2k probe → 8k resume). The THREE bundled resurrection levers over 6.6.0: reinit_label_rows [B-/I-dependent_locality], classifier_learning_rate 1.0e-3 (dedicated `classifier.*` param group), class_weights B-/I-dependent_locality 0.3→1.0 — plus the FOUR-locale corpus feed above.
training.tokenizer_splice: tokenizer_splice.py build-tokenizer --locales fr --per-file-cap 2000000 over OA fr/countrywide.csv (seed 42, 350k-line corpus, unigram vocab 24000) -> splice 2406 non-ASCII FR pieces into v0.7.1-nsplice (English byte-identity asserted). UNCHANGED from v240 (md5 04995524).
data_attribution: HM Land Registry — Price Paid Data (OGL v3.0): the `synth-gb-v1` training shard (800,000 rows) NEW in 6.7.0, and the `pair-index-gb.bin` placetype-pair index (`neural-weights-en-gb`, this same task). Crown copyright, attribution required.
LINZ-derived OpenAddresses NZ (CC-BY 4.0): the `synth-nz-v2` training shard (800,000 rows) NEW in 6.7.0.
OpenAddresses ES tuples (CC-BY per OA source): the `synth-es-pedania-v1` training shard (800,000 rows, below-municipio pedanía place names) NEW in 6.7.0.

BAN — Base Adresse Nationale (DINUM / IGN, Licence Ouverte 2.0): the `synth-fr-lieudit-v1` training shard (`nom_ld` lieu-dit field) NEW in 6.7.0, additional to the existing `synth-fr-fragment` shard (144,865 rows, 6.4.0).
Overture Maps Foundation — Addresses theme (CDLA-Permissive-2.0): the `overture-latam` CA/MX training shard (456,230 rows, 6.6.0). Attribution-only, not share-alike.
OpenAddresses CZ — ČÚZK RÚIAN (CC-BY 4.0): tokenizer-splice training text + the oa-cz coord eval sets
OpenAddresses PL — GUGiK / PRG (public, BDOT-derived): tokenizer-splice training text + the oa-pl coord eval sets
OpenAddresses SK — MINV/ZBGIS (CC-0/CC-BY per OA source): oa-sk coord eval set (free-rider validation)
OpenAddresses SI — GURS (CC-BY per OA source): oa-si coord eval set (free-rider validation)
See THIRD_PARTY_NOTICES.md for the standing OpenAddresses / GeoNames / WOF attribution.

architecture.params: ~29M (6-layer/384-hidden encoder + 73,143-piece multisplice embedding; init_from v264, 8k fine-tune w/ a non-exported span-boundary aux head)

requires.$comment: The SHIP-CONFIG this model was TRAINED against (#718). v263 adds `country` (the #1104 dedicated country soft-feed channel) to the prior anchor+gazetteer+conventions config. The canonical ProductionScorer (@mailwoman/neural's createScorer) reads this and FAILS CLOSED if a required channel isn't actually fed — closing the #566/#685 trap where a channel-trained model scored channel-OFF silently goes OOD. The server tier feeds anchor+gazetteer+country; the pocket tier is anchor-only (its capabilities country=0 by construction). Additive metadata only.

notes: v3.11.0-deploc-feed step-8000 ("feed-8k"), STAGED not promoted. Escalates the confirmed-but-skewed probe-2 mechanism (v3.10.1-gb-probe2: first-ever decode-layer dependent_locality emissions, GB 1/69, NZ 3/246) by feeding FOUR locales at once (GB+NZ+ES+FR-lieudit) instead of GB alone, holding total dep-loc stream density constant (~6.66%) while dropping the max single-locale share 20.2%→6.1% — the fix for probe-2's golden-us/FR-fragment/val skew costs. THE WIN, measured with the placetype-pair prior ON at its calibrated δ (a sibling arc, not this one): GB dependent_locality full-pipeline recall 67/69 (97.1%) — but the three-way ablation (below) shows this is almost entirely the PRIOR's contribution, not the checkpoint's own resurrected classifier row (raw decode-layer GB emission without the prior is 0/69 for feed-8k — see the ablation table). NZ, ES, and FR-lieudit have no equivalent prior to lean on and show the checkpoint's own resurrection is weak: NZ 0/246 (STILL zero, despite being the arc's original target locale), ES 7/65 (10.8%), FR-lieudit 1/80 (1.25%). All six pre-registered guards pass at feed-8k: golden us −0.5pp / fr +1.2pp (both within-or-favorable), FR-fragment bare-locality 0.988 (PASS, beats v385's own 0.983 — feed-2k FAILS this outright at 0.665, the reason feed-8k not feed-2k is the ratified ship checkpoint), val macro_f1 +1.29pp (favorable), 6 demo presets byte-identical, error-analysis no tag >2pp (worst: street −1.5pp). DIGIT GATE REVISION (operator-ratified 2026-07-23, Teffen Ellis): the pre-registered digit bar was originally read against the v3.10/v3.10.1 GB-probe lineage's OWN prior 8k checkpoint (bare-street-hn 0.902, a same-lineage probe-run number, not a real ship baseline) — that comparison is not apples-to-apples for THIS feed-8k checkpoint, which forked from v385 (6.6.0), not from a GB-probe ancestor. The bar is RESTATED against v385's own fresh-measured baseline (0.755, same session, same board): feed-8k scores 0.868, a +11.3pp gain over the correct baseline — PASS. The 0.902 probe-anchor number is retained here as historical context only, not as the active bar. GAUNTLET: FAILS (Task 8, 2026-07-23) — see phase field above; this is the one open ship blocker. CALIBRATION: the isotonic tables carry from the v5.3.0 lineage unchanged; opt-in, default output unaffected; re-fit is a tracked follow-up.

format.model: ONNX int8 dynamic (quantized from fp32, opset 17)
format.tokenizer: SentencePiece unigram, byte_fallback=true, vocab_size=73143 (v0.9.0-multisplice; ships WITH the model, both runtimes load it from this bundle)

calibration.method: isotonic-regression (PAVA) over per-span softmax confidence; OPT-IN via core/decoder createCalibrator
calibration.note: RE-FIT on v5.3.0 (2026-07-04): calibration.json + calibration-per-locale.json are now fitted on THIS model's confidence distribution (35,600 spans over the 8k held-out set), not carried from v4.x. The from-scratch model was under-confident (meanConf 0.913 vs acc 0.980, gap -0.067); the fit corrects held-out OA ECE 0.0698→0.0017 (overall 0.0677→0.0028, per-locale NL 0.166→0.000 / DE 0.114→0.003). Reproduce: collect-span-confidences.ts → fit-isotonic-calibration.py --model-version 5.3.0. Apply via @mailwoman/core/decoder's createCalibrator; default parse output is byte-stable when omitted.

base_relpath: models/quantized/model-v3110-deploc-feed-step-008000-int8.onnx under $MAILWOMAN_DATA_ROOT; training output on Modal volume mailwoman-training:/output-v3110-deploc-feed-s42/checkpoints/step-008000

gate_2026_07_23_v3110_deploc_feed_8k.$comment: Task 8 (placetype-pair-prior arc, ship-prep) full battery + Gauntlet, feed-8k vs v385 (6.6.0), all measured fresh this session (ONNX CPU int8). Full transcript: .superpowers/sdd/task-8-prep-report.md; the calibration + checkpoint-choice battery this gate builds on: .superpowers/sdd/task-7-report.md.
gate_2026_07_23_v3110_deploc_feed_8k.verdict: SHIP-BLOCKED — every pre-registered guard PASSES; the Gauntlet (a standing ship-gate requirement, not one of the six pre-registered guards) FAILS on its metamorphic layer. Not promoted pending operator triage.

fr_fragment_bare_locality.result: PASS (feed-8k); feed-2k FAILS outright — the deciding reason feed-8k is the ratified ship checkpoint over feed-2k

digit_bare_street_hn_gate_revision.$comment: Operator-ratified 2026-07-23 (Teffen Ellis). The pre-registered bar (0.902) was a same-lineage GB-probe checkpoint's own number, not a real ship baseline for a v385-forked checkpoint. Restated against v385's own fresh baseline, measured same session same board.
digit_bare_street_hn_gate_revision.old_bar_0_902_probe_anchor: historical context only, not the active bar — CI [0.831,0.897] excludes it

dependent_locality_ablation.$comment: The prior — not the checkpoint's own resurrected classifier row — carries nearly all of GB's measured recall. NZ/ES/FR-lieudit have no equivalent prior and show the checkpoint's own resurrection is weak-to-absent.

gate_2026_07_23_v3110_deploc_feed_8k.demo_presets: 6/6 byte-identical (both weights worlds — en-us control path AND en-gb weights with the prior wired)

gauntlet.regression: PASS (33/33 gated; 1 tracked known_fail now passes and should be promoted to status=pass — fr-chat-qui-peche-street-swallows-locality)
gauntlet.metamorphic: FAIL — NEW gate-failing violation: INV[comma-drop] '1600 Pennsylvania Ave NW, Washington DC 20500' -> comma-dropped form loses the rooftop resolution entirely (38.8977,-77.0365 -> 0,0). v385 HOLDS this exact case (confirmed same session). feed-2k independently FAILS the same layer with DIFFERENT violations on '350 Fifth Avenue, New York, NY' (283.5km off) — reads as a lineage-wide v3.11.0-deploc-feed robustness regression on NY/DC-class admin resolution, not a single-checkpoint fluke. Neither Task 6 nor Task 7 ran the Gauntlet; this is a NEW finding from Task 8.
gauntlet.held_out: PASS — z=0.00 (candidate not significantly worse), n=300 fresh FR/BAN draw
gauntlet.combined_verdict: FAIL — do not ship (until the metamorphic regression above is triaged)

eval.gate_2026_07_18_v710_camx: v6.0.0-shipped-baseline gate + golden 2pp gate (v385 vs shipped v381, channels-on, same run): PASS. Golden: exact-match 24.2% (1105 vs 1104, +1 row), country 89.8 vs 91.4 (−1.6, small-N 220/245, sub-threshold — the only mover >0.5pp down), region 78.1 vs 78.6 (−0.5), locality/postcode/street/house_number flat. NO tag >2pp down. Native-order held-out board: MX locality 74.0→82.7 (+8.7), CA neutral. Gauntlet (eval gauntlet --candidate): regression 33/33 PASS; metamorphic INV 62/63, DIR 3/3, BAND 16/21 — identical known-xfail profile to shipped v381 (the 1 INV[comma-drop] xfail is the pre-existing FR #1101 case; US comma-drop held). No new regression. Grade: docs/articles/evals/2026-07-18-v710-camx-latam-grade.md.
eval.gate_2026_07_17_v650_punct_digit: v6.0.0-shipped-baseline gate: PASS, 0 tags >2pp below shipped v310, +2.3pp us.country_homograph_f1 (only >2pp mover). Gauntlet: PASS — clear to ship (regression 33/33; metamorphic INV[comma-drop] 9/10 held — the US '1600 Pennsylvania Ave NW Washington DC' comma-free rooftop RESTORED, the FR 'Rue du Chevaleret' remains the tracked #1101 xfail; held-out FR/BAN n=300 not significantly worse). Digit ownership (NO board 3): bare-street-hn 0.693->0.733 (+4pp), bare-pc guard held 1.000. Grade: package-shaped --weights-cache (#718), int8 candidate vs int8 shipped, on 2026-07-17.

gate_2026_07_16_v640_fr_fragment.promotion_gate: v6.0.0-shipped-baseline gate: PASS 18/18, no revision, no relaxation. This spec was cut from the SHIPPED v264's own readings BEFORE v310 was graded, with per-metric noise margins (floor = shipped − max(1.0pp, 2 × the 95% binomial half-width)); it tightened 15 of 18 floors over the retired v5.3.0-family spec. The FR fragment board's targets and guards were pre-registered in the v3.1.0-fr-fragment config header and in baselines.json (profile fragment-fr-v264), registered against the shipped model before the run. 2pp pre-publish bar: clear on every tag. No trade was measured and none is claimed.
gate_docs: docs/articles/evals/2026-07-16-span-head-closed.md (the pre-registered follow-up: the span head does NOT compose with this shard)

fr_fragment_board.$note: Board 2 — BAN-sampled, 2800 fixtures, street surfaces disjoint from the training shard. Wilson 95% CIs. No target's interval overlaps its baseline.

bare_locality_counter_guard.$note: PRE-REGISTERED as the cell that might collapse by design. It held exactly, same interval — the model learned the distinction, not a new default.

global_parity_floor.$note: Board 1 — the guard. The FAIL verdicts eval parity prints on street/house_number are the v7 CAMPAIGN floors (0.90 / 0.97); shipped v264 fails them too. They are a target, not a regression gate.

gate_2026_07_16_v640_fr_fragment.known_open_not_addressed: Digit ownership — which tag owns a digit-bearing token (house_number vs postcode vs route number). Untouched by this shard and explicitly out of its scope. Parity PRECISION (new in this cycle — the half no floor could previously see, since every floor did `if (!goldValues?.length) continue`) reads postcode 25/249 = 0.100, street 12/54 = 0.222, house_number 6/175 = 0.034 emitted where the gold has none. v310 moves this by −1 row. Tracked as its own workstream; whether these become formal floors is an open operator decision.

gate_2026_07_04_v530_full_family.promotion_gate: v5.3.0-family gate (clone of v5.2.0-nordic + the fr.postcode 99.3→99.2 revision): PASS with the three operator-approved trades stated above. The promote decision ran on the 2026-07-03 RE-GATE of the archived candidate under the #942-floored stack — fresh dumps BOTH arms, same stack (the #942-D1 stack-drift lesson): 6/6 ni legs PASS; the SI wrong-city re-gate bar (≤+2pp) failed at +2.7pp and was adjudicated at the fork, not silently absorbed. Original 100k battery: 15/17 floors (the two above), mask-regression PASS, zero NaN.
gate_docs: #901 (the campaign + the promote fork), #949 (the FR fragmentation this fixes)
gate_2026_07_04_v530_full_family.ni_legs: US-2k p50 3.27 identical, resolve 1.000 both; FR-3k p50 1.30 identical (deconfounded same-stack); SE +4.6pp resolve; Abo 0.12km vs 859km

gate_2026_07_02_v510_vocab_splice.promotion_gate: #884 gates ALL PASS, pre-registered before measurement (gate log: /mnt/playpen/mailwoman-data/scratch-825/gate-v193/RESULTS.md; evidence comments on #884). US-2k non-inferiority passed at the strongest possible level — the int8 candidate's per-row dump is byte-identical to shipped v4.15.0's. CZ/PL/SK/SI improve gates: wrong-city diff CIs wholly negative, resolved-p50 improve CIs wholly negative, resolve-rate floors held. Functional eyeball: CZ churn (103 fixed / 43 newly-wrong) diagnosed as a pre-existing locality-truncation→namesake residual exposed where the baseline won by garbage-parse fluke — NOT a parse regression (10/10 rows read). The int8 SLO overage (+3.8 MB) was an explicit operator accept (four locales for the same bytes).

wrong_city_resolved_gt20km_n1k.$note: reservoir-sampled 1k-row OA coord sets (oa-{cz,pl,sk,si}-coord-1k.jsonl), graded parse→resolve→great-circle, int8 ship artifact

us_2k_non_inferiority.row_dump: BYTE-IDENTICAL to shipped v4.15.0 int8 (diff 0 everywhere)

gate_2026_07_02_v510_vocab_splice.tokenization_examples: Vysoká 4→1 piece, Grudziądz 6→1, Świętokrzyska 9→1, Čistá 5→2; SK fertility −51.9%, SI −35.2% on diacritic-bearing names with zero SK/SI-specific pieces

gate_2026_06_25_v415_anchor_absorption.promotion_gate: v4.15.0-boundary gate PASS after a stated, operator-approved revision of two postcode floors (us.postcode 97→95, fr.postcode 99.5→99.3). The #723 fix (SLICE-H anchor-ON 20→100) trades −2pp us.postcode LABEL-F1 on the rare leading-5-digit-postcode (VT E911) case. The trade is COORDINATE-INVISIBLE — proven by the non-circular oa-resolver-eval below, not asserted. Every other floor holds with margin (us.locality 76.7/62.2, us.street 82.3/74, us.region 88.6/80.1, de.native_locality 89.3/83.8, arena.perturb 78/71); mask-regression gate (#718) PASS; int8↔fp32 delta 0. No silent drift: the revision is in scripts/eval/gates/v4.15.0-boundary.json with the reason, mirrored here and in the promotion doc.

slice_h_anchor_on.$note: leading-5-digit house# correctly labeled house_number (not postcode) with the anchor ON — the #723 production bug

oa_resolver_eval_us.$note: non-circular: real OA gov lat/lon vs resolved admin centroid, full ship-config (anchor+gazetteer+suppress)

vt_locality_match.$note: VT = the E911 leading-postcode state where the postcode label trade lives — FLAT at the coordinate

label_deltas_coordinate_invisible: us.postcode -2.0 (95.0) — rare leading-5-digit-postcode VT E911 case; VT coordinate flat (92.6=92.6)

gate_2026_06_19_fr_admin_split.promotion_gate: FR centroid gate PASS — surpasses v1.5.0 on the SHIPPED assembled anchor-ON coordinate (held-out FR golden, 3000 disjoint communes): coord p50 42.5→2.2 km, mean 175→105.5 km (−40%), région-emit 39.6→99.6%. US assembled coordinate FLAT (oa-resolver-eval, 2000 OA-US rows: locality-match 97.5→97.6, p50 3.3=3.3). Two label deltas trip the 2pp pre-publish gate, BOTH coordinate-invisible and operator-approved (#728): us.locality −2.2 (precision-only, 0 recall misses) and fr.country −3.5 (resolver given --default-country FR). No silent drift — both stated here + on #728 + the night postmortem.
flagged_deltas_coordinate_invisible: us.locality -2.2 (precision-only — 0 recall misses, spurious-FP on gold-locality-absent rows)
fr.country -3.5 (resolver given --default-country FR, never reaches the coordinate; abandoned v1.8.1 country-shard targeted this)

per_component_int8_shipconfig.$comment: per-locale-f1 anchor-ON matched pair vs v1.5.0 (postmortem guardrail tables). The unfolded per-tag F1 (split affix, intersections, etc.) is the authoritative `capabilities` block below. Tags off the FR-shard path (us intersections/unit, de native_locality, arena.perturb) were not re-measured for v1.8.0 — the single training variable was the FR admin-split shard and the architecture is identical to v1.5.0.

vs_1_5_0.fr_coord_p50: 42.5 → 2.2 km (−95%) — the win; first model in the arc to beat v1.5.0 on the shipped coordinate

files_md5.$comment: The SHIPPED artifact's md5 — this is the identity `neural-weights-en-us/scripts/link-dev-weights.ts`'s #397 guard and `mailwoman eval gauntlet`'s self-check (#1024 drift guard) verify the dev symlink / materialized tarball against, repo-wide. Deliberately LEFT AT v385 (6.6.0): 6.7.0 is a STAGED, NOT-PROMOTED candidate (see phase) — flipping this to the candidate's md5 before the operator actually promotes would silently repoint every other branch's `yarn test`/local dev at unshipped bytes. The candidate's own identity is `staged_candidate_files_md5` below; only THAT flips to feed-8k. Bump this block (and the two DEFAULT_* paths in link-dev-weights.ts) together, in lockstep, at actual promotion time.

staged_candidate_files_md5.$comment: 6.7.0's candidate artifact (feed-8k) — NOT yet the shipped identity above. int8, v3.11.0-deploc-feed step-008000, init_from v385 (6.6.0) step-8000. Tokenizer UNCHANGED from the shipped v385 tokenizer (same v0.9.0-multisplice bytes — no tokenizer change this cycle).

capabilities.$carried_forward_comment: NOT regenerated for 6.7.0 (Task 8 ship-prep did not re-run `mailwoman eval capability-manifest`) — these numbers are the 6.6.0/v385 measurement, carried forward as the best available reference. Flagged explicitly rather than silently reused; a fresh manifest is a tracked follow-up if 6.7.0 promotes.
capabilities.$comment: Per-tier (server=anchor+gazetteer; pocket=anchor-only) × address-system × tag capability manifest (#718/#719). maskOffF1 = measured per-tag exact-match F1 with the conventions mask OFF; maskOnF1 = the same with mask ON (recorded only for tags some codex forbiddenTags row suppresses — the loader's delta-gate consults only those). createScorer FAILS CLOSED when a conventions row forbids a tag with maskOffF1 − maskOnF1 > 0.05 (the mask provably destroys a real capability). Generated by `mailwoman eval capability-manifest` against the v1.5.0 int8.
